steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side
steps.content: Before the blank: “officially patented on April 10,” is within a relative clause. After the blank: “[blank] only model had been suspiciously destroyed...” is an independent clause needing “Page's” as subject.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: An independent clause requires a period (starting a new sentence) or semicolon/conjunction (continuing one sentence), not a comma alone. Choice D uses “1906—roughly six weeks earlier.” — the period creates a sentence boundary, allowing “Page's only model had been...” to begin as a new, complete sentence. The em dashes set off “roughly six weeks earlier” as explanatory. Choices A and C create comma splices, while B has a formatting error. That matches choice D — D is correct.
